\section{The \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{HighOrderStats}} Class and its Functionalities}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:the-highorderstats-class-and-its-functionalities}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{HighOrderStats} class is used to compute and analyze higher order statistics from the time series data. It provides methods for calculating log\sphinxhyphen{}likelihoods and performing Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE).
\index{HighOrderStats (class in xtremes.topt)@\spxentry{HighOrderStats}\spxextra{class in xtremes.topt}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.HighOrderStats}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{class\DUrole{w,w}{  }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.topt.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{HighOrderStats}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{TimeSeries}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
HighOrderStats class for calculating and analyzing high\sphinxhyphen{}order statistics of time series data.


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:notes}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This class provides functionality for calculating Probability Weighted Moment (PWM) estimators and
Maximum Likelihood (ML) estimators from a given TimeSeries object.


\subsection{Methods}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id8}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{get\_PWM\_estimation():}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculate the PWM estimators for the time series data.

\sphinxlineitem{get\_ML\_estimation(initParams=’auto’, FrechetOrGEV = ‘Frechet’, option=1, estimate\_pi=False):}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculate the ML estimators for the time series data.

\end{description}


\subsection{Attributes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id9}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{TimeSeries}{[}TimeSeries{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The TimeSeries object containing the time series data.

\sphinxlineitem{high\_order\_stats}{[}list{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
List of high\sphinxhyphen{}order statistics extracted from the TimeSeries object.

\sphinxlineitem{blockmaxima}{[}list{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
List of block maxima derived from the high\sphinxhyphen{}order statistics.

\sphinxlineitem{gamma\_true}{[}float{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
True gamma parameter of the GEV distribution derived from the TimeSeries object.

\sphinxlineitem{PWM\_estimators}{[}PWM\_estimators{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Instance of PWM\_estimators class for calculating PWM estimators.

\sphinxlineitem{ML\_estimators}{[}ML\_estimators{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Instance of ML\_estimators class for calculating ML estimators.

\end{description}


\subsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:example}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Create a TimeSeries object}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{ts} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{TimeSeries}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{n}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{distr}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{GEV}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{correlation}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{ARMAX}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{modelparams}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.5}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ts}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.6}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Simulate time series data}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{ts}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{simulate}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{rep}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{seeds}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{42}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{123}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{456}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{789}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1011}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Initialize HighOrderStats object}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{hos} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{HighOrderStats}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ts}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Calculate PWM estimators}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{hos}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}PWM\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Calculate ML estimators}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{hos}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}ML\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{initParams}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{auto}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{r}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id10}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{initParams}{[}str or array\sphinxhyphen{}like, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxlineitem{FrechetOrGEV}{[}str, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Whether to fit the Frechet or GEV distribution.

\end{description}

\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id11}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function performs maximum likelihood estimation based on either the Frechet or GEV distribution.

\section{Examples}

\item {}

\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{xtremes}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{topt} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{HighOrderStats}
\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}

\PYG{n}{hos\PYGZus{}data} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{array}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{0.2}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.3}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{0.4}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{0.5}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.4}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{0.6}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{hos} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{HighOrderStats}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{hos\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{log\PYGZus{}likelihood} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{hos}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{log\PYGZus{}likelihood}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{gamma}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{mu}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{sigma}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{r}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Log Likelihood:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{log\PYGZus{}likelihood}\PYG{p}{)}

\section{The \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{Data}} Class and its Functionalities}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:the-data-class-and-its-functionalities}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{Data} class is used to handle and manipulate real data for analysis.
\index{Data (class in xtremes.topt)@\spxentry{Data}\spxextra{class in xtremes.topt}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.Data}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{class\DUrole{w,w}{  }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.topt.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{Data}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{values}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures

Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
A class for analyzing data with block maxima and high order statistics.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class provides methods to extract block maxima and high order statistics from a given dataset. 
It also supports Maximum Likelihood (ML) estimation for the parameters of either the Frechet or GEV distributions.


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id13}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{values}{[}list or numpy.ndarray{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The dataset from which block maxima and high order statistics are extracted. This should be a 1D array 
or list of values representing the time series or data.

\end{description}


\subsection{Attributes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id14}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{values}{[}list or numpy.ndarray{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The dataset on which operations are performed.

\sphinxlineitem{len}{[}int{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The length of the dataset.

\sphinxlineitem{blockmaxima}{[}list{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
List to store the block maxima extracted from the dataset.

\sphinxlineitem{bm\_indices}{[}list{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
List of indices corresponding to the positions of the block maxima in the original dataset.

\sphinxlineitem{high\_order\_stats}{[}list{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
List to store the high order statistics extracted from the dataset.

\sphinxlineitem{ML\_estimators}{[}ML\_estimators\_data{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Object that stores and handles the ML estimation results for the Frechet or GEV parameters.

\end{description}


\subsection{Methods}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id15}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{get\_blockmaxima(block\_size=2, stride=’DBM’):}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracts block maxima from the dataset.

\sphinxlineitem{get\_HOS(orderstats=2, block\_size=2, stride=’DBM’):}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Extracts high order statistics from the dataset.

\sphinxlineitem{get\_ML\_estimation(r=None, FrechetOrGEV=’GEV’):}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes ML estimations for the Frechet or GEV parameters.

\end{description}


\subsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id16}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Initialize the Data object with a dataset}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{data} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Data}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{2.5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{3.1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{1.7}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{4.6}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{5.3}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{2.2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{6.0}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Extract block maxima using a block size of 2}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{data}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}blockmaxima}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{block\PYGZus{}size}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{stride}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{DBM}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{data}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{blockmaxima}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Extract second\PYGZhy{}highest order statistics (HOS) with the same block size and stride}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{data}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}HOS}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{orderstats}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{block\PYGZus{}size}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{stride}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{DBM}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{data}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{high\PYGZus{}order\PYGZus{}stats}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Perform ML estimation using the extracted HOS, choosing between Frechet and GEV}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{data}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}ML\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{FrechetOrGEV}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{GEV}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{data}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{ML\PYGZus{}estimators}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{values}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\index{get\_HOS() (xtremes.topt.Data method)@\spxentry{get\_HOS()}\spxextra{xtremes.topt.Data method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.Data.get_HOS}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_HOS}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{orderstats}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{block\_size}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{stride}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'DBM'}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Extract high order statistics (HOS) from the dataset.

\sphinxAtStartPar
High order statistics refer to statistics of interest beyond the maximum (e.g., second\sphinxhyphen{}highest, 
third\sphinxhyphen{}highest). This method extracts these statistics from blocks of the dataset.


\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id17}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{orderstats}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The order of the statistic to extract (e.g., 2 for second\sphinxhyphen{}highest). Default is 2.

\sphinxlineitem{block\_size}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The size of blocks from which to extract the statistics. Default is 2.

\sphinxlineitem{stride}{[}str or int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The type of stride to use when extracting blocks. Choose from:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘SBM’: Sliding Block Maxima (step size 1)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘DBM’: Disjoint Block Maxima (non\sphinxhyphen{}overlapping blocks)
\sphinxhyphen{} int: Specifies the step size directly.
Default is ‘DBM’.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:returns}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{None}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The high order statistics are stored in the \sphinxtitleref{high\_order\_stats} attribute.

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{get\_ML\_estimation() (xtremes.topt.Data method)@\spxentry{get\_ML\_estimation()}\spxextra{xtremes.topt.Data method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.Data.get_ML_estimation}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_ML\_estimation}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{r}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{FrechetOrGEV}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'GEV'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{bounds}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute Maximum Likelihood (ML) estimations for the Frechet or GEV parameters.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This method computes ML estimators for the parameters of either the Frechet or GEV distribution based 
on the high order statistics extracted from the data. If no high order statistics are available, it 
will first extract them.


\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id18}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{r}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The number of order statistics to use in the ML estimation. If not specified, it uses all the extracted statistics.

\sphinxlineitem{FrechetOrGEV}{[}str, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The type of distribution to use for the ML estimation. Choose between ‘Frechet’ and ‘GEV’. Default is ‘GEV’.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id19}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{None}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The ML estimators are stored in the \sphinxtitleref{ML\_estimators} attribute.

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{get\_blockmaxima() (xtremes.topt.Data method)@\spxentry{get\_blockmaxima()}\spxextra{xtremes.topt.Data method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.Data.get_blockmaxima}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_blockmaxima}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{block\_size}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{stride}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'DBM'}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Extract block maxima from the dataset.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Block maxima are the largest values in each block of the dataset, where the block size 
and the stride (step size) determine how the blocks are divided.


\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id20}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{block\_size}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The size of blocks for maxima extraction. Default is 2.

\sphinxlineitem{stride}{[}str or int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The type of stride to use when extracting blocks. Choose from:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘SBM’: Sliding Block Maxima (step size 1)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘DBM’: Disjoint Block Maxima (non\sphinxhyphen{}overlapping blocks)
\sphinxhyphen{} int: Specifies the step size directly.
Default is ‘DBM’.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Returns}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The block maxima and their corresponding indices are stored in the \sphinxtitleref{blockmaxima} 
and \sphinxtitleref{bm\_indices} attributes.

\end{description}

\section{The \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{PWM\_estimators}} Class}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:the-pwm-estimators-class}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{PWM\_estimators} class is used to compute Probability Weighted Moment (PWM) estimators.
\index{PWM\_estimators (class in xtremes.topt)@\spxentry{PWM\_estimators}\spxextra{class in xtremes.topt}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.PWM_estimators}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{class\DUrole{w,w}{  }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.topt.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PWM\_estimators}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{TimeSeries}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculates Probability Weighted Moment (PWM) estimators from block maxima and computes statistics and confidence intervals for the GEV parameters.


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id22}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This class provides methods to compute the Probability Weighted Moment (PWM) estimators and convert them into Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) 
distribution parameters (gamma, mu, sigma). It also allows users to compute confidence intervals for the estimated parameters and assess 
the statistical properties (mean, variance, bias, mean squared error) of the estimators compared to a true gamma value. Visualization options 
are provided to plot the estimators and confidence intervals.


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id23}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param TimeSeries}
\sphinxAtStartPar
TimeSeries object
TimeSeries object containing block maxima or high order statistics.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Attributes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id24}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{attribute blockmaxima}
\sphinxAtStartPar
numpy.ndarray
Array of block maxima extracted from the TimeSeries object.

\sphinxlineitem{attribute values}
\sphinxAtStartPar
numpy.ndarray
Array containing the PWM estimators (\(\gamma, \mu, \sigma\)) for each block maxima series.

\sphinxlineitem{attribute statistics}
\sphinxAtStartPar
dict
Dictionary containing statistics (mean, variance, bias, mse) and confidence intervals of the PWM estimators.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Methods}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id25}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{method get\_PWM\_estimation()}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute PWM estimators for each block maxima series and convert them into GEV parameters.

\sphinxlineitem{method get\_statistics(gamma\_true)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute statistics of the PWM estimators using a true gamma value.

\sphinxlineitem{method get\_CIs(alpha=0.05, method=’symmetric’)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute confidence intervals (CIs) for the GEV parameters using either symmetric quantiles or minimal width intervals.

\sphinxlineitem{method plot(param=’gamma’, show\_CI=True, show\_true=True, filename=None)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Plot the PWM estimators for the GEV parameters (gamma, mu, sigma) with options to display confidence intervals and save the plot as an image.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Raises}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id26}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{raises ValueError}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If block maxima or high order statistics are not available in the TimeSeries object.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Examples}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id27}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{TimeSeries} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{TimeSeries}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimators} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimators}

\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{pwm} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimators}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ts}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Initialize PWM\PYGZus{}estimators object}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{pwm}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}PWM\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Compute PWM estimators}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{pwm}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}statistics}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.1}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Compute statistics with true gamma value 0.1}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{pwm}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}CIs}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.05}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{method}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{minimal\PYGZus{}width}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Compute confidence intervals}

\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{pwm}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{statistics}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Print computed statistics}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}gamma\PYGZus{}mean\PYGZsq{}: 0.25, \PYGZsq{}gamma\PYGZus{}variance\PYGZsq{}: 0.005, \PYGZsq{}gamma\PYGZus{}bias\PYGZsq{}: 0.02, \PYGZsq{}gamma\PYGZus{}mse\PYGZsq{}: 0.01,}
\PYG{g+go}{ \PYGZsq{}mu\PYGZus{}mean\PYGZsq{}: 1.15, \PYGZsq{}mu\PYGZus{}variance\PYGZsq{}: 0.04, \PYGZsq{}sigma\PYGZus{}mean\PYGZsq{}: 0.9, \PYGZsq{}sigma\PYGZus{}variance\PYGZsq{}: 0.02,}
\PYG{g+go}{ \PYGZsq{}gamma\PYGZus{}CI\PYGZsq{}: (0.2, 0.5), \PYGZsq{}mu\PYGZus{}CI\PYGZsq{}: (1.1, 1.5), \PYGZsq{}sigma\PYGZus{}CI\PYGZsq{}: (0.8, 1.0)\PYGZcb{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{get\_CIs() (xtremes.topt.PWM\_estimators method)@\spxentry{get\_CIs()}\spxextra{xtremes.topt.PWM\_estimators method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.PWM_estimators.get_CIs}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_CIs}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{alpha}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.05}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{method}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'symmetric'}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute confidence intervals (CIs) for the GEV parameters using different methods.


\subsubsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id28}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function calculates confidence intervals (CIs) for the Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) parameters (gamma, mu, and sigma) estimated from 
Probability\sphinxhyphen{}Weighted Moments (PWM). The user can choose between two methods for computing the confidence intervals:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{‘symmetric’}: This method uses the quantiles of the distribution of parameter estimates to compute symmetric confidence intervals.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{‘minimal\_width’}: This method finds the interval with minimal width that contains the desired proportion (1 \sphinxhyphen{} alpha) of the sorted parameter estimates.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
For each block maxima series, confidence intervals for the GEV shape (gamma), location (mu), and scale (sigma) parameters are calculated. The results 
are stored in the \sphinxtitleref{self.statistics} dictionary, with keys ‘gamma\_CI’, ‘mu\_CI’, and ‘sigma\_CI’ corresponding to the computed confidence intervals.


\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id29}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param alpha}
\sphinxAtStartPar
float, optional
Significance level for the confidence intervals (default is 0.05, for a 95\% CI).

\sphinxlineitem{param method}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str, optional
Method for computing the confidence intervals. Options are:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘symmetric’: Uses quantiles to compute symmetric CIs (default).
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘minimal\_width’: Computes the minimal width interval containing (1 \sphinxhyphen{} alpha) of the estimates.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsubsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id30}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}Estimators}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{timeseries\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}PWM\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}CIs}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.05}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{method}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{minimal\PYGZus{}width}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{statistics}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}gamma\PYGZus{}CI\PYGZsq{}: (0.2, 0.5), \PYGZsq{}mu\PYGZus{}CI\PYGZsq{}: (1.1, 1.5), \PYGZsq{}sigma\PYGZus{}CI\PYGZsq{}: (0.8, 1.0)\PYGZcb{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id31}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{None}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The results are stored in \sphinxtitleref{self.statistics}, which contains the confidence intervals for each GEV parameter.

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{get\_PWM\_estimation() (xtremes.topt.PWM\_estimators method)@\spxentry{get\_PWM\_estimation()}\spxextra{xtremes.topt.PWM\_estimators method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.PWM_estimators.get_PWM_estimation}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_PWM\_estimation}}}{}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute Probability\sphinxhyphen{}Weighted Moment (PWM) estimators and convert them into GEV parameters.


\subsubsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id32}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function iterates over each block maxima series, computes the Probability\sphinxhyphen{}Weighted Moments (PWMs), and converts the PWMs into the 
Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) distribution parameters: shape (gamma), location (mu), and scale (sigma). The function utilizes the 
\sphinxtitleref{misc.PWM\_estimation} function to calculate the PWMs and then applies \sphinxtitleref{misc.PWM2GEV} to convert these moments into GEV parameters.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The results for each block maxima series are stored in the \sphinxtitleref{self.values} attribute, which is a NumPy array where each row corresponds to 
the GEV parameters {[}gamma, mu, sigma{]} for a specific block maxima series.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This function clears any previously stored values in \sphinxtitleref{self.values} before appending new estimates.


\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id33}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
None


\subsubsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id34}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimators}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{timeseries\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}PWM\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{values}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{array([[0.2, 1.1, 0.8],}
\PYG{g+go}{    [0.3, 1.2, 0.9]])}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id35}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{None}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The results are stored in \sphinxtitleref{self.values}, a NumPy array containing the estimated GEV parameters for each block maxima series.

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{get\_statistics() (xtremes.topt.PWM\_estimators method)@\spxentry{get\_statistics()}\spxextra{xtremes.topt.PWM\_estimators method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.PWM_estimators.get_statistics}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_statistics}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{gamma\_true}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute statistics of the PWM estimators using the true gamma value.


\subsubsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id36}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function calculates various statistical measures (mean, variance, bias, and mean squared error) for the estimated 
Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) shape parameter (gamma) compared to a provided true value (\sphinxtitleref{gamma\_true}). It also computes 
the mean and variance for the location (mu) and scale (sigma) parameters across all block maxima series.
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Mean Squared Error (MSE)}: Measures the average of the squares of the differences between the estimated and true gamma values.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Bias}: Represents the systematic deviation of the estimated gamma values from the true gamma value.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Variance}: Describes the spread of the estimated gamma values around their mean.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
If only one block maxima series is available, a warning is raised since variance cannot be computed.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The computed statistics are stored in the \sphinxtitleref{self.statistics} dictionary with the following keys:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘gamma\_mean’, ‘gamma\_variance’, ‘gamma\_bias’, ‘gamma\_mse’
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘mu\_mean’, ‘mu\_variance’
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘sigma\_mean’, ‘sigma\_variance’


\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id37}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param gamma\_true}
\sphinxAtStartPar
float
The true value of the GEV shape parameter (gamma) used to compute bias and MSE.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsubsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id38}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimators}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{timeseries\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}PWM\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}statistics}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{gamma\PYGZus{}true}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.2}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{statistics}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}gamma\PYGZus{}mean\PYGZsq{}: 0.25, \PYGZsq{}gamma\PYGZus{}variance\PYGZsq{}: 0.005, \PYGZsq{}gamma\PYGZus{}bias\PYGZsq{}: 0.02, \PYGZsq{}gamma\PYGZus{}mse\PYGZsq{}: 0.01,}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZsq{}mu\PYGZus{}mean\PYGZsq{}: 1.15, \PYGZsq{}mu\PYGZus{}variance\PYGZsq{}: 0.04, \PYGZsq{}sigma\PYGZus{}mean\PYGZsq{}: 0.9, \PYGZsq{}sigma\PYGZus{}variance\PYGZsq{}: 0.02\PYGZcb{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id39}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{None}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The results are stored in \sphinxtitleref{self.statistics}, containing the calculated statistics for gamma, mu, and sigma.

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

Plot the PWM estimators and confidence intervals for the GEV parameters.


\subsubsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id40}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function generates a plot showing the Probability\sphinxhyphen{}Weighted Moment (PWM) estimators for the Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) parameters 
(gamma, mu, sigma) computed from block maxima. The user can choose to display confidence intervals (CIs) for each parameter.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The plot is saved as a PNG image if the \sphinxtitleref{save} parameter is set to True.


\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id41}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param param}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str, optional
GEV parameter to plot (default is ‘gamma’).

\sphinxlineitem{param show\_CI}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool, optional
Flag indicating whether to display confidence intervals (default is True).

\sphinxlineitem{param show}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool, optional
Flag indicating whether to display the plot.

\sphinxlineitem{param save}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool, optional
Flag indicating whether to save the plot as a PNG image (default is False).

\sphinxlineitem{param filename}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str, optional
Name of the PNG file to save the plot (default is None).

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsubsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id42}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimators}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{timeseries\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}PWM\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}CIs}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.05}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{method}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{minimal\PYGZus{}width}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{plot}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{show\PYGZus{}CI}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{show\PYGZus{}true}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{filename}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimation.png}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id43}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{None}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The plot is displayed in the console and saved as a PNG image if the \sphinxtitleref{save} parameter is set to True.

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\index{automatic\_parameter\_initialization() (in module xtremes.topt)@\spxentry{automatic\_parameter\_initialization()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.topt}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.automatic_parameter_initialization}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.topt.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{automatic\_parameter\_initialization}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{PWM\_estimators}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{corr}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{ts}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.5}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Automatic parameter initialization for ML estimation.


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id44}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function is designed for initializing parameters for maximum likelihood estimation (ML) in statistical models.
It automatically computes the probability weighted moment (PWM) estimators and adjusts them based on the specified
correlation type (‘ARMAX’, ‘IID’, etc.). The ‘ts’ parameter is used to control the strength of temporal dependence
in the model.


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id45}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param PWM\_estimators}
\sphinxAtStartPar
list or numpy.array
Probability weighted moment estimators.

\sphinxlineitem{param corr}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str
Correlation type for the model. Supported values are ‘ARMAX’, ‘IID’, etc.

\sphinxlineitem{param ts}
\sphinxAtStartPar
float, optional
Time series parameter controlling the strength of temporal dependence (default is 0.5).

\sphinxlineitem{return}
\sphinxAtStartPar
numpy.ndarray
Initial parameters for ML estimation.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{See also}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:see-also}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
misc.PWM\_estimation : Function for computing probability weighted moment estimators.


\subsection{Examples}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id46}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{test\PYGZus{}xtremes} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{misc}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}est} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{misc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimators}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{data}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{init\PYGZus{}params} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{automatic\PYGZus{}parameter\PYGZus{}initialization}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}est}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{ARMAX}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ts}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.8}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}



\section{The \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{ML\_estimators}}, \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{Frechet\_ML\_estimators}} and \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{ML\_estimators\_data}} Classes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:the-ml-estimators-frechet-ml-estimators-and-ml-estimators-data-classes}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{ML\_estimators}, \sphinxtitleref{Frechet\_ML\_estimators}, and \sphinxtitleref{ML\_estimators\_data} classes are used for performing Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE) and handling the results.
\index{ML\_estimators (class in xtremes.topt)@\spxentry{ML\_estimators}\spxextra{class in xtremes.topt}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.ML_estimators}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{class\DUrole{w,w}{  }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.topt.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ML\_estimators}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{TimeSeries}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum Likelihood Estimators (MLE) for GEV parameters.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class calculates Maximum Likelihood Estimators (MLE) for the parameters of the Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) distribution using the method of maximum likelihood estimation.


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id47}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{TimeSeries}{[}TimeSeries{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The TimeSeries object containing the data for which MLE estimators will be calculated.

\end{description}


\subsection{Attributes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id48}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{values}{[}numpy.ndarray{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
An array containing the MLE estimators for each set of high order statistics.

\sphinxlineitem{statistics}{[}dict{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A dictionary containing statistics computed from the MLE estimators.

\end{description}


\subsection{Methods}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id49}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\_\_len\_\_()}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Returns the number of MLE estimators calculated.

\sphinxlineitem{get\_ML\_estimation(PWM\_estimators=None, initParams=’auto’, option=1, estimate\_pi=False)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes the MLE estimators for the GEV parameters.

\sphinxlineitem{get\_statistics(gamma\_true)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes statistics from the MLE estimators.

\end{description}


\subsection{Examples}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id50}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{hos} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{TimeSeries}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ML\PYGZus{}estimators}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimators}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{blockmaxima\PYGZus{}data} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{random}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{normal}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{loc}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{scale}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{size}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{high\PYGZus{}order\PYGZus{}stats\PYGZus{}data} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{random}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{normal}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{loc}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{scale}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{size}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{ts} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{TimeSeries}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{blockmaxima}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{blockmaxima\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{high\PYGZus{}order\PYGZus{}stats}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{high\PYGZus{}order\PYGZus{}stats\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{corr}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{IID}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ts}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.5}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{pwm} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimators}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ts}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{pwm}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}PWM\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{ml} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ML\PYGZus{}estimators}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ts}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{ml}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}ML\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimators}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{pwm}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{ml}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}statistics}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{gamma\PYGZus{}true}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.1}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ML Estimators:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ml}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{values}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+se}{\PYGZbs{}n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Statistics:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ml}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{statistics}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{get\_CIs() (xtremes.topt.ML\_estimators method)@\spxentry{get\_CIs()}\spxextra{xtremes.topt.ML\_estimators method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.ML_estimators.get_CIs}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_CIs}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{alpha}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.05}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{method}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'symmetric'}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute confidence intervals (CIs) for the GEV parameters using different methods.


\subsubsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id51}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function calculates confidence intervals (CIs) for the Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) parameters (gamma, mu, and sigma) 
estimated from Maximum Likelihood Estimators (MLE). The user can choose between two methods for computing the confidence intervals:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{‘symmetric’}: This method uses the quantiles of the distribution of parameter estimates to compute symmetric confidence intervals.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{‘minimal\_width’}: This method finds the interval with minimal width that contains the desired proportion (1 \sphinxhyphen{} alpha) of the sorted parameter estimates.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
For each block maxima series, confidence intervals for the GEV shape (gamma), location (mu), and scale (sigma) parameters are calculated. The results 
are stored in the \sphinxtitleref{self.statistics} dictionary, with keys ‘gamma\_CI’, ‘mu\_CI’, and ‘sigma\_CI’ corresponding to the computed confidence intervals.


\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id52}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param alpha}
\sphinxAtStartPar
float, optional
Significance level for the confidence intervals (default is 0.05, for a 95\% CI).

\sphinxlineitem{param method}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str, optional
Method for computing the confidence intervals. Options are:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘symmetric’: Uses quantiles to compute symmetric CIs (default).
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘minimal\_width’: Computes the minimal width interval containing (1 \sphinxhyphen{} alpha) of the estimates.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsubsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id53}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ML\PYGZus{}stimator}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{timeseries\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}PWM\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}CIs}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.05}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{method}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{minimal\PYGZus{}width}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{statistics}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}gamma\PYGZus{}CI\PYGZsq{}: (0.2, 0.5), \PYGZsq{}mu\PYGZus{}CI\PYGZsq{}: (1.1, 1.5), \PYGZsq{}sigma\PYGZus{}CI\PYGZsq{}: (0.8, 1.0)\PYGZcb{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id54}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{None}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The results are stored in \sphinxtitleref{self.statistics}, which contains the confidence intervals for each GEV parameter.

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{get\_ML\_estimation() (xtremes.topt.ML\_estimators method)@\spxentry{get\_ML\_estimation()}\spxextra{xtremes.topt.ML\_estimators method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.ML_estimators.get_ML_estimation}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_ML\_estimation}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{PWM\_estimators}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{initParams}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'auto'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{r}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute Maximum Likelihood (ML) estimators for each series of high order statistics within the \sphinxtitleref{ML\_estimators} class.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This method fits the Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) distribution to each series of high order statistics
using Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE) by optimizing the log\sphinxhyphen{}likelihood function.


\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id55}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{PWM\_estimators}{[}PWM\_estimators object, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
An object containing PWM (Probability Weighted Moments) estimators. This is used for initializing the 
parameters in the ML estimation if \sphinxtitleref{initParams} is set to ‘auto’. Required if \sphinxtitleref{initParams} is ‘auto’.

\sphinxlineitem{initParams}{[}str or numpy.ndarray, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Initial parameters for the ML estimation. If ‘auto’, the initial parameters will be computed automatically 
using the \sphinxtitleref{PWM\_estimators} object. If a NumPy array is provided, these will be used as initial parameter values.
Default is ‘auto’.

\sphinxlineitem{r}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The number of order statistics to calculate the log\sphinxhyphen{}likelihood on. If not specified, all provided 
order statistics will be used.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id56}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{None}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This method updates the \sphinxtitleref{self.values} attribute of the \sphinxtitleref{ML\_estimators} object with the estimated parameters 
(gamma, mu, sigma) for each series of high order statistics.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Raises}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id57}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{ValueError}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If \sphinxtitleref{initParams} is set to ‘auto’ and no \sphinxtitleref{PWM\_estimators} are provided, a ValueError is raised.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id58}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
This method performs Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE) to fit the Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) distribution to the high order statistics within the \sphinxtitleref{ML\_estimators} class.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The method uses optimization techniques such as Nelder\sphinxhyphen{}Mead (and optionally COBYLA) to minimize the negative log\sphinxhyphen{}likelihood.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
If \sphinxtitleref{initParams} is set to ‘auto’, the initial parameters for the optimization are derived using the \sphinxtitleref{PWM\_estimators} object.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The optimization results (gamma, mu, sigma) are stored in the \sphinxtitleref{self.values} list for each series of high order statistics.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

Compute statistics of the ML estimators using a true \(\gamma\) value.

\sphinxAtStartPar
float

\index{plot() (xtremes.topt.ML\_estimators method)@\spxentry{plot()}\spxextra{xtremes.topt.ML\_estimators method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.ML_estimators.plot}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{plot}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{param}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'gamma'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{show\_CI}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{show\_true}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{filename}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Plot the ML estimators and confidence intervals for the GEV parameters.


\subsubsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id60}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function generates a plot showing the Maximum Likelihood estimators for the Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) parameters 
(gamma, mu, sigma) computed from block maxima. The user can choose to display confidence intervals (CIs) for each parameter

\sphinxAtStartPar
The plot is saved as a PNG image if the \sphinxtitleref{save} parameter is set to True.


\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id61}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param param}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str, optional
GEV parameter to plot (default is ‘gamma’).

\sphinxlineitem{param show\_CI}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool, optional
Flag indicating whether to display confidence intervals (default is True).

\sphinxlineitem{param show}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool, optional
Flag indicating whether to display the plot.

\sphinxlineitem{param save}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool, optional
Flag indicating whether to save the plot as a PNG image (default is False).

\sphinxlineitem{param filename}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str, optional
Name of the PNG file to save the plot (default is None).

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsubsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id62}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ML\PYGZus{}estimators}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{timeseries\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}ML\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}CIs}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.05}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{method}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{minimal\PYGZus{}width}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{plot}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{show\PYGZus{}CI}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{show\PYGZus{}true}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{filename}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimation.png}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id63}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{None}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The plot is displayed in the console and saved as a PNG image if the \sphinxtitleref{save} parameter is set to True.

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\index{Frechet\_ML\_estimators (class in xtremes.topt)@\spxentry{Frechet\_ML\_estimators}\spxextra{class in xtremes.topt}}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{class\DUrole{w,w}{  }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.topt.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{Frechet\_ML\_estimators}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{TimeSeries}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum Likelihood Estimators (MLE) for Frechet parameters.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class calculates Maximum Likelihood Estimators (MLE) for the parameters of the 2\sphinxhyphen{}parameter Frechet 
distribution using the method of maximum likelihood estimation on a series of high order statistics.


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id64}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{TimeSeries}{[}TimeSeries{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The TimeSeries object containing the data (high order statistics) for which MLE estimators will be calculated.

\end{description}


\subsection{Attributes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id65}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{values}{[}numpy.ndarray{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
An array containing the MLE estimators (alpha, sigma) for each set of high order statistics.

\sphinxlineitem{statistics}{[}dict{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A dictionary containing computed statistics from the MLE estimators such as mean, variance, bias, and MSE.

\end{description}


\subsection{Methods}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id66}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\_\_len\_\_()}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Returns the number of MLE estimators calculated.

\sphinxlineitem{get\_ML\_estimation(PWM\_estimators=None, initParams=’auto’, r=None)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes the MLE estimators for the Frechet parameters (alpha, sigma).

\sphinxlineitem{get\_statistics(alpha\_true)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes statistics (mean, variance, bias, and MSE) of the MLE estimators using a true alpha value.

\end{description}


\subsection{Examples}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id67}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{hos} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{TimeSeries}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{Frechet\PYGZus{}ML\PYGZus{}estimators}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimators}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{blockmaxima\PYGZus{}data} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{random}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{normal}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{loc}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{scale}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{size}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{high\PYGZus{}order\PYGZus{}stats\PYGZus{}data} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{random}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{normal}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{loc}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{scale}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{size}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{ts} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{TimeSeries}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{blockmaxima}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{blockmaxima\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{high\PYGZus{}order\PYGZus{}stats}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{high\PYGZus{}order\PYGZus{}stats\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{corr}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{IID}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ts}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.5}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{pwm} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimators}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ts}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{pwm}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}PWM\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{ml} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Frechet\PYGZus{}ML\PYGZus{}estimators}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ts}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{ml}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}ML\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimators}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{pwm}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{ml}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}statistics}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{alpha\PYGZus{}true}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.1}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ML Estimators:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ml}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{values}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+se}{\PYGZbs{}n}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Statistics:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ml}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{statistics}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{get\_CIs() (xtremes.topt.Frechet\_ML\_estimators method)@\spxentry{get\_CIs()}\spxextra{xtremes.topt.Frechet\_ML\_estimators method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.Frechet_ML_estimators.get_CIs}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_CIs}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{alpha}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.05}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{method}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'symmetric'}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute confidence intervals (CIs) for the Frechet parameters using different methods.


\subsubsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id68}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function calculates confidence intervals (CIs) for the Frechet parameters (alpha and sigma) 
estimated from Maximum Likelihood Estimators (MLE). The user can choose between two methods for computing the confidence intervals:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{‘symmetric’}: This method uses the quantiles of the distribution of parameter estimates to compute symmetric confidence intervals.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{‘minimal\_width’}: This method finds the interval with minimal width that contains the desired proportion (1 \sphinxhyphen{} alpha) of the sorted parameter estimates.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
For each block maxima series, confidence intervals for the shape (alpha) and scale (sigma) parameters are calculated. The results 
are stored in the \sphinxtitleref{self.statistics} dictionary, with keys ‘gamma\_CI’, ‘mu\_CI’, and ‘sigma\_CI’ corresponding to the computed confidence intervals.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not get confused with alpha being the significance level as well as the shape parameter of the Frechet distribution.


\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id69}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param alpha}
\sphinxAtStartPar
float, optional
Significance level for the confidence intervals (default is 0.05, for a 95\% CI).

\sphinxlineitem{param method}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str, optional
Method for computing the confidence intervals. Options are:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘symmetric’: Uses quantiles to compute symmetric CIs (default).
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘minimal\_width’: Computes the minimal width interval containing (1 \sphinxhyphen{} alpha) of the estimates.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsubsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id70}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ML\PYGZus{}stimator}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{timeseries\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}PWM\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}CIs}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.05}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{method}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{minimal\PYGZus{}width}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{statistics}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id71}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{None}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The results are stored in \sphinxtitleref{self.statistics}, which contains the confidence intervals for each GEV parameter.

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{get\_ML\_estimation() (xtremes.topt.Frechet\_ML\_estimators method)@\spxentry{get\_ML\_estimation()}\spxextra{xtremes.topt.Frechet\_ML\_estimators method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.Frechet_ML_estimators.get_ML_estimation}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_ML\_estimation}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{PWM\_estimators}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{initParams}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'auto'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{r}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{weights}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute ML estimators (alpha, sigma) for each high order statistics series using Frechet distribution.


\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id72}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{PWM\_estimators}{[}PWM\_estimators object, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PWM\_estimators object containing PWM estimators for initializing parameters. Required if \sphinxtitleref{initParams} 
is set to ‘auto’.

\sphinxlineitem{initParams}{[}str or numpy.ndarray, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Initial parameters for ML estimation. ‘auto’ to calculate automatically using PWM estimators.
If a numpy array is provided, these will be used as initial parameter values. Default is ‘auto’.

\sphinxlineitem{r}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Number of order statistics to calculate the log\sphinxhyphen{}likelihood on. If not specified, all provided order 
statistics will be used.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id73}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{None}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Updates the \sphinxtitleref{self.values} attribute with the estimated parameters (alpha, sigma) for each series 
of high order statistics.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Raises}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id74}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{ValueError}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If \sphinxtitleref{initParams} is set to ‘auto’ and no \sphinxtitleref{PWM\_estimators} are provided.

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{get\_statistics() (xtremes.topt.Frechet\_ML\_estimators method)@\spxentry{get\_statistics()}\spxextra{xtremes.topt.Frechet\_ML\_estimators method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.Frechet_ML_estimators.get_statistics}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_statistics}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{alpha\_true}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute statistics (mean, variance, bias, and MSE) of the ML estimators using the true \(\alpha\) value.


\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id75}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{alpha\_true}{[}float{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The true value of \(\alpha\) to calculate bias, MSE, and other statistics.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id76}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{None}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Updates the \sphinxtitleref{self.statistics} dictionary with calculated statistics such as mean, variance, bias, 
and MSE for both \(\alpha\) and \(\sigma\).

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id77}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The statistics include:
\sphinxhyphen{} Mean and variance for the estimated alpha and sigma values.
\sphinxhyphen{} Bias and mean squared error (MSE) for the alpha estimates.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{plot() (xtremes.topt.Frechet\_ML\_estimators method)@\spxentry{plot()}\spxextra{xtremes.topt.Frechet\_ML\_estimators method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.Frechet_ML_estimators.plot}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{plot}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{param}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'alpha'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{show\_CI}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{show\_true}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{filename}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Plot the ML estimators and confidence intervals for the GEV parameters.


\subsubsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id78}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function generates a plot showing the Maximum Likelihood estimators for the Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) parameters 
(gamma, mu, sigma) computed from block maxima. The user can choose to display confidence intervals (CIs) for each parameter

\sphinxAtStartPar
The plot is saved as a PNG image if the \sphinxtitleref{save} parameter is set to True.


\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id79}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param param}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str, optional
GEV parameter to plot (default is ‘gamma’).

\sphinxlineitem{param show\_CI}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool, optional
Flag indicating whether to display confidence intervals (default is True).

\sphinxlineitem{param show}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool, optional
Flag indicating whether to display the plot.

\sphinxlineitem{param save}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool, optional
Flag indicating whether to save the plot as a PNG image (default is False).

\sphinxlineitem{param filename}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str, optional
Name of the PNG file to save the plot (default is None).

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsubsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id80}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimators}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{timeseries\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}PWM\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}CIs}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.05}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{method}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{minimal\PYGZus{}width}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{estimator}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{plot}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{show\PYGZus{}CI}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{show\PYGZus{}true}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{filename}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimation.png}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id81}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{None}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The plot is displayed in the console and saved as a PNG image if the \sphinxtitleref{save} parameter is set to True.

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\index{log\_likelihood() (in module xtremes.topt)@\spxentry{log\_likelihood()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.topt}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_topt:xtremes.topt.log_likelihood}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.topt.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{log\_likelihood}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{high\_order\_statistics}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{gamma}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{mu}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{sigma}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{r}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculate the GEV log likelihood based on the two highest order statistics.


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id82}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{high\_order\_statistics}{[}numpy.ndarray{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A 2D array where each row contains the two highest order statistics for each observation.

\sphinxlineitem{gamma}{[}float, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The shape parameter (γ) for the Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) distribution. Default is 0.

\sphinxlineitem{mu}{[}float, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The location parameter (μ) for the GEV distribution. Default is 0.

\sphinxlineitem{sigma}{[}float, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The scale parameter (σ) for the GEV distribution. Must be positive. Default is 1.

\sphinxlineitem{r}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The number of order statistics to calculate the log\sphinxhyphen{}likelihood on. If not specified, it uses all provided statistics.

\end{description}


\subsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id83}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{float}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The calculated log likelihood.

\end{description}


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id84}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function computes the log likelihood using the two highest order statistics and supports both the classical 
Gumbel case (γ = 0) and the generalized case (γ ≠ 0).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{high\_order\_statistics} array should be structured with the two highest order statistics per observation as rows.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The shape parameter γ controls the tail behavior of the distribution. When γ = 0, the distribution becomes the Gumbel type.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{r} parameter controls how many order statistics are used for the likelihood calculation, typically \sphinxtitleref{r=2} for two order statistics.

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id85}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{hos} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{array}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{0.2}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.3}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{0.4}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{0.5}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.4}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{0.6}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{log\PYGZus{}likelihood}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{hos}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{gamma}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{mu}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{sigma}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{r}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZhy{}7.494890426732856}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{Frechet\_log\_likelihood() (in module xtremes.topt)@\spxentry{Frechet\_log\_likelihood()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.topt}}

\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.topt.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{Frechet\_log\_likelihood}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{high\_order\_statistics}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{alpha}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{sigma}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{r}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{weights}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculate the 2\sphinxhyphen{}parameter Frechet log likelihood based on the highest order statistics. 
The calculation can be done using either the joint likelihood of the top two order statistics or the product of their marginals.


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id86}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{high\_order\_statistics}{[}numpy.ndarray{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A 2D array where each row contains the two highest order statistics for each observation.

\sphinxlineitem{alpha}{[}float, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The shape parameter (α) for the Frechet distribution. Default is 1. Controls the tail behavior of the distribution.

\sphinxlineitem{sigma}{[}float, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The scale parameter (σ) for the Frechet distribution. Must be positive. Default is 1.

\sphinxlineitem{r}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The number of order statistics to calculate the log\sphinxhyphen{}likelihood on. If not specified, it uses all provided statistics.

\sphinxlineitem{weights}{[}numpy.ndarray, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
ABM as stride provides weights. If they are provided, use them instead of counts

\end{description}


\subsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id87}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{float}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The calculated log likelihood for the given data under the Frechet distribution.

\end{description}


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id88}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function computes the log likelihood using the two highest order statistics from each observation, with a focus on the Frechet distribution.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The shape parameter \sphinxtitleref{alpha} determines the heaviness of the tail in the distribution, and the scale parameter \sphinxtitleref{sigma} must be strictly positive.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{high\_order\_statistics} array should be structured such that each row represents the two highest order statistics for an observation.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The function can either calculate the joint likelihood of the top two order statistics or consider the product of their marginals, depending on the values used.

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id89}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{hos} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{array}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{1.0}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{1.5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{2.0}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{1.2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{2.2}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{2.0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{3.0}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{Frechet\PYGZus{}log\PYGZus{}likelihood}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{hos}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{sigma}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{1.5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{r}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZhy{}15.78467219003245}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Run maximum likelihood estimation (ML) for a given time series file.


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id90}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function reads a time series from a file and performs ML estimation for the specified correlation type, GEV shape parameter, block sizes,
and stride type. It iterates over each block size, extracts block maxima, computes high order statistics, and performs ML estimation.
The results are stored in a dictionary with the GEV shape parameter as the key and ML estimation results for each block size as the value.


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id91}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param file}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str
Path to the file containing the time series data.

\sphinxlineitem{param corr}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str, optional
Correlation type for the model (default is ‘IID’).

\sphinxlineitem{param gamma\_true}
\sphinxAtStartPar
float, optional
True value of the GEV shape parameter (default is 0).

\sphinxlineitem{param block\_sizes}
\sphinxAtStartPar
list, optional
List of block sizes for extracting block maxima (default is {[}5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50{]}).

\sphinxlineitem{param stride}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str, optional
Stride type for block maxima extraction. Options are ‘SBM’ (sliding block maxima) or ‘DBM’ (default block maxima) (default is ‘SBM’).

\sphinxlineitem{param r}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, optional
Number of orderstatistics to calculate the log\sphinxhyphen{}likelihood on. If not specified, use all provided

\sphinxlineitem{param estimate\_pi}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool, optional
Flag indicating whether to estimate the pi parameter (default is False).

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id92}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{run\PYGZus{}ML\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{timeseries\PYGZus{}data.pkl}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{corr}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{ARMAX}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{gamma\PYGZus{}true}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{block\PYGZus{}sizes}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{stride}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{DBM}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{option}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{estimate\PYGZus{}pi}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZob{}0.5: \PYGZob{}10: HighOrderStats\PYGZus{}object, 20: HighOrderStats\PYGZus{}object, 30: HighOrderStats\PYGZus{}object\PYGZcb{}\PYGZcb{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id93}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{return}
\sphinxAtStartPar
dict
Dictionary containing ML estimation results for each block size.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}
