\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Run multiple maximum likelihood estimations (ML) for a range of GEV shape parameter values.


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id94}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function performs ML estimation for a range of GEV shape parameter values specified in ‘gamma\_trues’ and aggregates the results into a single dictionary.
It iterates over each gamma value, calls the ‘run\_ML\_estimation’ function, and collects the results. If ‘parallelize’ is set to True, it runs the estimations
concurrently using asyncio.


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id95}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param file}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str
Path to the file containing the time series data.

\sphinxlineitem{param corr}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str, optional
Correlation type for the model (default is ‘IID’).

\sphinxlineitem{param gamma\_trues}
\sphinxAtStartPar
numpy.ndarray, optional
Array of GEV shape parameter values to perform ML estimation for (default is np.arange(\sphinxhyphen{}4, 5, 1)/10).

\sphinxlineitem{param block\_sizes}
\sphinxAtStartPar
list, optional
List of block sizes for extracting block maxima (default is {[}5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50{]}).

\sphinxlineitem{param stride}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str, optional
Stride type for block maxima extraction. Options are ‘SBM’ (sliding block maxima) or ‘DBM’ (default block maxima) (default is ‘SBM’).

\sphinxlineitem{param r}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, optional
Number of orderstatistics to calculate the log\sphinxhyphen{}likelihood on. If not specified, use all provided

\sphinxlineitem{param estimate\_pi}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool, optional
Flag indicating whether to estimate the pi parameter (default is False).

\sphinxlineitem{param parallelize}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool, optional
Flag indicating whether to parallelize the ML estimations using asyncio (default is False).

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_topt:id96}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{return}
\sphinxAtStartPar
dict
Dictionary containing ML estimation results for each gamma value and block size.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {}

\chapter{Reference: Bootstrap}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:reference-bootstrap}}\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module computes a Bootstrap procedure on disjoint or sliding block maxima.


\section{Overview}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:overview}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{xtremes.bootstrap} module provides tools for performing bootstrap procedures on block maxima. It includes classes and functions for extracting block maxima, resampling, and estimating parameters using Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE).


\section{Classes}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:classes}}

\section{The \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{FullBootstrap}} Class}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:the-fullbootstrap-class}}\index{FullBootstrap (class in xtremes.bootstrap)@\spxentry{FullBootstrap}\spxextra{class in xtremes.bootstrap}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:xtremes.bootstrap.FullBootstrap}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{class\DUrole{w,w}{  }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.bootstrap.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{FullBootstrap}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{initial\_sample}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{bs}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{10}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{stride}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'DBM'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{dist\_type}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'Frechet'}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
A class to perform bootstrapping of Maximum Likelihood Estimates (MLE) for Fréchet or GEV distributions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class performs block maxima extraction from an initial sample using either disjoint or sliding blocks.
It applies a bootstrap resampling procedure to estimate the variability of the MLE parameters for the specified
distribution type (Fréchet or GEV). The bootstrap method is parallelized for efficiency and supports reproducibility
through optional seed setting.


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:parameters}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{initial\_sample}{[}list or numpy.ndarray{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The initial dataset from which block maxima will be extracted and bootstrapped.

\sphinxlineitem{bs}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Block size for the block maxima extraction. Default is 10.

\sphinxlineitem{stride}{[}\{‘DBM’, ‘SBM’\}, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Stride type for block maxima extraction:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘DBM’ (Disjoint Block Maxima): Non\sphinxhyphen{}overlapping blocks.
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘SBM’ (Sliding Block Maxima): Overlapping blocks.
Default is ‘DBM’.

\sphinxlineitem{dist\_type}{[}\{‘Frechet’, ‘GEV’\}, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Distribution type to estimate the parameters for:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘Frechet’: Estimate parameters for the 2\sphinxhyphen{}parametric Fréchet distribution.
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘GEV’: Estimate parameters for the 3\sphinxhyphen{}parametric Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) distribution.
Default is ‘Frechet’.

\end{description}


\subsection{Attributes}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:attributes}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{circmaxs}{[}list{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The block maxima extracted from the initial sample using the specified block size and stride.

\sphinxlineitem{data}{[}hos.Data{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{hos.Data} object containing the original dataset and its MLE results.

\sphinxlineitem{MLEvals}{[}numpy.ndarray{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The MLE estimates from the original dataset before bootstrapping.

\sphinxlineitem{values}{[}numpy.ndarray{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
MLE estimates for each bootstrap sample after running the \sphinxtitleref{run\_bootstrap} method.

\sphinxlineitem{statistics}{[}dict{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dictionary containing summary statistics (mean and standard deviation) of the bootstrap estimates.

\end{description}


\subsection{Methods}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:methods}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{run\_bootstrap(num\_bootstraps=100, set\_seeds=False, max\_workers=1)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Runs the bootstrap procedure in parallel and calculates the MLE estimates for each bootstrap sample.

\end{description}


\subsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:example}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{sample} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{random}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{rand}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{bootstrap} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{FullBootstrap}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{sample}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{bs}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{stride}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{DBM}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{dist\PYGZus{}type}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{Frechet}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{bootstrap}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{run\PYGZus{}bootstrap}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{num\PYGZus{}bootstraps}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{set\PYGZus{}seeds}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{max\PYGZus{}workers}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{bootstrap}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{statistics}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{mean}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Mean of bootstrap estimates}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{bootstrap}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{statistics}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{std}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}   \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Standard deviation of bootstrap estimates}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{get\_CI() (xtremes.bootstrap.FullBootstrap method)@\spxentry{get\_CI()}\spxextra{xtremes.bootstrap.FullBootstrap method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:xtremes.bootstrap.FullBootstrap.get_CI}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_CI}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{alpha}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.05}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{method}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'bootstrap'}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute the confidence interval (CI) for the Maximum Likelihood Estimate (MLE) parameters 
based on bootstrap samples.


\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:id1}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{alpha}{[}float, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Significance level for the confidence interval. Default is 0.05, 
corresponding to a 95\% confidence interval.

\sphinxlineitem{method}{[}str, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Method to compute the confidence interval. Two options are available:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘symmetric’: The confidence interval is computed using the symmetric quantiles.
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘minimal\_width’: The confidence interval is computed by finding the minimal\sphinxhyphen{}width 
interval that contains (1 \sphinxhyphen{} alpha) proportion of the bootstrap distribution.
The default is ‘symmetric’.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:returns}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{numpy.ndarray}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A 2D array with shape (n\_parameters, 2) containing the lower and upper bounds of 
the confidence interval for each parameter. The first column represents the lower 
bounds, and the second column represents the upper bounds.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:notes}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The confidence intervals are based on bootstrap estimates of the MLE parameters, which 
means the confidence intervals are derived from the empirical distribution of the parameter 
estimates obtained from multiple bootstrap samples.

\sphinxAtStartPar
There are two methods available for calculating the confidence intervals:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘symmetric’: This method takes the alpha/2 and (1 \sphinxhyphen{} alpha/2) quantiles of the bootstrap 
distribution for each parameter. It is based on the assumption that the distribution 
is approximately symmetric and works well when the bootstrap distribution is roughly normal.
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘minimal\_width’: This method identifies the interval with the minimal width that contains 
(1 \sphinxhyphen{} alpha) proportion of the bootstrap samples. It is particularly useful when the 
bootstrap distribution is skewed or not symmetric.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{plot\_bootstrap() (xtremes.bootstrap.FullBootstrap method)@\spxentry{plot\_bootstrap()}\spxextra{xtremes.bootstrap.FullBootstrap method}}

\pysigstopsignatures

Plot the bootstrap distribution for a specified parameter.

\index{run\_bootstrap() (xtremes.bootstrap.FullBootstrap method)@\spxentry{run\_bootstrap()}\spxextra{xtremes.bootstrap.FullBootstrap method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:xtremes.bootstrap.FullBootstrap.run_bootstrap}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{run\_bootstrap}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{num\_bootstraps}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{100}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{set\_seeds}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{max\_workers}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Run the bootstrap resampling procedure in parallel.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This method resamples the block maxima dataset, estimates the MLE parameters for each bootstrap sample,
and computes summary statistics (mean and standard deviation) of the bootstrap estimates. The computation
is parallelized using \sphinxtitleref{ProcessPoolExecutor} with an adjustable number of worker processes.


\subsubsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:id4}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{num\_bootstraps}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Number of bootstrap samples to generate. Default is 100.

\sphinxlineitem{set\_seeds}{[}bool, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If True, sets the random seed for reproducibility in each bootstrap iteration. Default is False.

\sphinxlineitem{max\_workers}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum number of worker processes to use for parallelization. Default is 1 (no parallelism).
Set to \sphinxtitleref{None} to use all available CPU cores.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:id5}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{None}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Results are stored in the \sphinxtitleref{values} attribute and summary statistics in the \sphinxtitleref{statistics} attribute.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:id6}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{bootstrap}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{run\PYGZus{}bootstrap}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{num\PYGZus{}bootstraps}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{500}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{set\PYGZus{}seeds}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{max\PYGZus{}workers}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{bootstrap}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{statistics}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{mean}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Access the mean of bootstrap estimates}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{bootstrap}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{statistics}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{std}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}   \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Access the standard deviation of bootstrap estimates}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}



\section{Functions}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:functions}}

\section{The \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{circmax}} Function}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:the-circmax-function}}\index{circmax() (in module xtremes.bootstrap)@\spxentry{circmax()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.bootstrap}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:xtremes.bootstrap.circmax}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.bootstrap.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{circmax}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{sample}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{bs}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{10}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{stride}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'DBM'}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Extract the block maxima (BM) from a given sample using different stride methods.


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:id7}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{sample}{[}numpy.ndarray{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A 1D array containing the sample from which block maxima will be extracted.

\sphinxlineitem{bs}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The block size (number of observations per block) used to divide the sample for block maxima extraction. Default is 10.

\sphinxlineitem{stride}{[}\{‘DBM’, ‘SBM’\}, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The stride method used for extracting block maxima:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘DBM’ (Disjoint Block Maxima): Extracts maxima from non\sphinxhyphen{}overlapping blocks.
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘SBM’ (Sliding Block Maxima): Extracts maxima using overlapping blocks. Default is ‘DBM’.

\end{description}


\subsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:id8}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{numpy.ndarray}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A 1D or 2D array containing the block maxima extracted from the sample. The result depends on the stride method used:
\sphinxhyphen{} For ‘DBM’, returns a 1D array of block maxima.
\sphinxhyphen{} For ‘SBM’, returns a 2D array where each row contains the block maxima extracted from overlapping blocks.

\end{description}


\subsection{Raises}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:raises}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{ValueError}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If an invalid stride method is specified.

\end{description}


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:id9}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
‘DBM’ (Disjoint Block Maxima) extracts block maxima from non\sphinxhyphen{}overlapping blocks of size \sphinxtitleref{bs}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
‘SBM’ (Sliding Block Maxima) creates overlapping blocks, effectively increasing the number of block maxima compared to ‘DBM’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
In the ‘SBM’ setting, the circmax() method introduced by Bücher and Staud 2024 is used.

\end{itemize}


\subsection{References}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:references}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bücher, A., \& Staud, T. (2024). Bootstrapping Estimators based on the Block Maxima Method. 
arXiv preprint arXiv:2409.05529.


\subsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:id10}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{sample} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{array}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{6}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{7}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{8}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{9}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{circmax}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{sample}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{bs}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{stride}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{DBM}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{array([5, 10])}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{circmax}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{sample}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{bs}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{stride}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{SBM}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{array([[3, 6, 9],}
\PYG{g+go}{       [4, 7, 10]])}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\section{The \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{bootstrap\_worker}} Function}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:the-bootstrap-worker-function}}\index{bootstrap\_worker() (in module xtremes.bootstrap)@\spxentry{bootstrap\_worker()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.bootstrap}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:xtremes.bootstrap.bootstrap_worker}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.bootstrap.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{bootstrap\_worker}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{args}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Auxiliary function to perform a single bootstrap resampling and MLE estimation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This function is designed to be used in parallelized bootstrap procedures. It takes arguments for a single 
bootstrap iteration, performs resampling on the given block maxima, estimates MLE parameters using the 
specified distribution type, and returns the results.


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:id20}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{args}{[}tuple{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A tuple containing the following elements:
\sphinxhyphen{} idx (int): The iteration index, used for setting the random seed if \sphinxtitleref{set\_seeds} is True.
\sphinxhyphen{} set\_seeds (bool): Whether to set the random seed for reproducibility.
\sphinxhyphen{} circmaxs (list or numpy.ndarray): The block maxima dataset to be resampled.
\sphinxhyphen{} aggregate\_boot (callable): A function to aggregate the resampled data.
\sphinxhyphen{} ML\_estimators\_data (callable): A function or class to compute MLE parameters on the aggregated data.
\sphinxhyphen{} dist\_type (str): The distribution type for MLE estimation (‘Frechet’ or ‘GEV’).

\end{description}


\subsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:id21}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{numpy.ndarray}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The MLE parameter estimates for the current bootstrap sample.

\end{description}


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:id22}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function is designed to be compatible with \sphinxtitleref{ProcessPoolExecutor} or other parallel processing tools.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The random seed is set per iteration to ensure reproducibility when \sphinxtitleref{set\_seeds} is True.

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:id23}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{args} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{circmaxs}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{aggregate\PYGZus{}boot}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ML\PYGZus{}estimators\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{GEV}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{bootstrap\PYGZus{}worker}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{args}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{array([param1, param2, param3])  \PYGZsh{} Example output for GEV distribution}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}



\section{Examples}
\label{\detokenize{api_bootstrap:examples}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Here are some examples of how to use the \sphinxtitleref{xtremes.bootstrap} module:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{FullBootstrap Class}:
\begin{quote}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{xtremes}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{bootstrap} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{FullBootstrap}

\PYG{n}{sample} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{random}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{rand}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{bootstrap} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{FullBootstrap}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{sample}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{bs}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{stride}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{DBM}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{dist\PYGZus{}type}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{Frechet}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\chapter{Reference: Miscellaneous}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:reference-miscellaneous}}\label{\detokenize{api_misc::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module is a collection for non\sphinxhyphen{}specialized, frequently used or basic functions.


\section{Overview}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:overview}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{xtremes.miscellaneous} module provides a variety of utility functions that can be used across different parts of your project. These functions are designed to be general\sphinxhyphen{}purpose and can help simplify common tasks.


\section{Basic Functions}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:basic-functions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The following functions are basic utility functions provided by the \sphinxtitleref{xtremes.miscellaneous} module:
\index{sigmoid() (in module xtremes.miscellaneous)@\spxentry{sigmoid()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.miscellaneous}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_misc:xtremes.miscellaneous.sigmoid}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.miscellaneous.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{sigmoid}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{x}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute the sigmoid function for the given input.


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:parameters}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param x}
\sphinxAtStartPar
array\_like
The input value or array.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:returns}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{return}
\sphinxAtStartPar
numpy.ndarray
The sigmoid of the input array. It has the same shape as \sphinxtitleref{x}.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:notes}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The sigmoid function is defined as:
\begin{equation*}
\begin{split}\text{sigmoid}(x) = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-x}}\end{split}
\end{equation*}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
It maps any real\sphinxhyphen{}valued number to the range (0, 1).

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:example}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{x} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{array}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{sigmoid}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{x}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{array([0.11920292, 0.26894142, 0.5       , 0.73105858, 0.88079708])}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{invsigmoid() (in module xtremes.miscellaneous)@\spxentry{invsigmoid()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.miscellaneous}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_misc:xtremes.miscellaneous.invsigmoid}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.miscellaneous.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{invsigmoid}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{y}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute the inverse sigmoid function for the given input.


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id1}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param y}
\sphinxAtStartPar
float or array\_like
The input value or array, representing probabilities in the range {[}0, 1{]}.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id2}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{return}
\sphinxAtStartPar
float or numpy.ndarray
The inverse sigmoid of the input value or array.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Raises}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:raises}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{ValueError}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If the input value is outside the range {[}0, 1{]}.

\end{description}


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id3}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The inverse sigmoid function finds the input value(s) that would produce the given output probability.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
It is the inverse of the sigmoid function: \sphinxtitleref{invsigmoid(sigmoid(x)) = x}.

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id4}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{y} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{array}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{0.5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{0.9}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{invsigmoid}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{y}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{array([\PYGZhy{}1.3652469 ,  0.        ,  1.3652469 ])}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{mse() (in module xtremes.miscellaneous)@\spxentry{mse()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.miscellaneous}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_misc:xtremes.miscellaneous.mse}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.miscellaneous.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{mse}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{gammas}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{gamma\_true}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute Mean Squared Error, Variance, and Bias of estimators.


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id5}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes the Mean Squared Error (MSE), Variance, and Bias of a set of estimators
given the true (theoretical) value. This function is intended for estimating the GEV
shape parameter \(\gamma\), but works for other estimators as well.
\begin{equation*}
\begin{split}\mathrm{MSE}(\hat\gamma) &:= \frac{1}{n-1}\sum_{i=1}^n \left(\hat\gamma_i-\gamma\right)^2 \\
\mathrm{Var}(\hat\gamma) &:= \frac{1}{n-1}\sum_{i=1}^n \left(\hat\gamma_i-\overline\gamma\right)^2 \\
\mathrm{Bias}(\hat\gamma) &:= \frac{n}{n-1} \left(\gamma-\overline\gamma\right)^2\end{split}
\end{equation*}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Here, \(\overline\gamma\) denotes the mean.
\begin{equation*}
\begin{split}\overline\gamma := \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n \gamma_i\end{split}
\end{equation*}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Also note that:
\begin{equation*}
\begin{split}\mathrm{MSE} := \mathrm{Bias} + \mathrm{Var}\end{split}
\end{equation*}

\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id6}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param gammas}
\sphinxAtStartPar
array\_like
Estimated values.

\sphinxlineitem{param gamma\_true}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int or float
True (theoretical) parameter.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id7}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{return}
\sphinxAtStartPar
tuple{[}float{]}
MSE, variance, and bias.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Raises}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id8}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{raise test\_xtremes.miscellaneous.warning}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{len(gammas)==1}}. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NaN}}s are returned.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}



\section{Examples}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:examples}}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Sigmoid Function}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]

\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{sigmoid}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{x}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Sigmoid Result:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\item {}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]

\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_misc:xtremes.miscellaneous.GEV_pdf}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.miscellaneous.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{GEV\_pdf}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{x}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{gamma}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{mu}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{sigma}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute the Probability Density Function (PDF) of the Generalized Extreme Value distribution.


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id9}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes the probability density function of the Generalized Extreme Value distribution:
\begin{equation*}
\begin{split}g(x) = \frac{\exp\left(-\left(1+\gamma \frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}\right)^{-1/\gamma}\right)\cdot
        \left(1+\gamma \frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}\right)^{-1-1/\gamma}}{\sigma}\end{split}
\end{equation*}

\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id10}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param x}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, float, list or numpy.ndarray
GEV argument \(x\in \mathbb{R}\).

\sphinxlineitem{param gamma}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, float, list or numpy.ndarray, optional
GEV shape parameter \(\gamma\in \mathbb{R}\). Default is 0.

\sphinxlineitem{param mu}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, float, list or numpy.ndarray, optional
GEV location parameter \(\mu\in \mathbb{R}\). Default is 0.

\sphinxlineitem{param sigma}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, float, list or numpy.ndarray, optional
GEV scale parameter \(\sigma>0\). Default is 1.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id11}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{return}
\sphinxAtStartPar
numpy.ndarray or float
The Probability Density Function values corresponding to the input \sphinxtitleref{x}.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id12}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{x} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{array}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{GEV\PYGZus{}pdf}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{x}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{gamma}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{mu}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{sigma}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{array([0.17603266, 0.23254416, 0.28556358, 0.33477888, 0.37960368])}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{GEV\_cdf() (in module xtremes.miscellaneous)@\spxentry{GEV\_cdf()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.miscellaneous}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_misc:xtremes.miscellaneous.GEV_cdf}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.miscellaneous.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{GEV\_cdf}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{x}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{gamma}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{mu}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{sigma}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{theta}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute the Cumulative Density Function (CDF) of the Generalized Extreme Value distribution.


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id13}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes the cumulative density function of the Generalized Extreme Value distribution:
\begin{equation*}
\begin{split}G_{\gamma,\mu,\sigma}(x) = \exp\left(-\left(1+\gamma \frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}\right)^{-1/\gamma}\right).\end{split}
\end{equation*}
\sphinxAtStartPar
For \(\gamma=0\), the term can be interpreted as the limit \(\lim_{\gamma\to 0}\):
\begin{equation*}
\begin{split}G_{0,\mu,\sigma}(x) = \exp\left(-\exp\left(-\frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}\right)\right).\end{split}
\end{equation*}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function also allows the usage of an extremal index, another parameter relevant when
dealing with stationary time series and its extreme values:
\begin{equation*}
\begin{split}G_{\gamma,\mu,\sigma,\vartheta}(x) = \exp\left(-\vartheta\left(1+\gamma \frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}\right)^{-1/\gamma}\right).\end{split}
\end{equation*}

\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id14}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param x}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, float, list or numpy.ndarray
GEV argument \(x\in \mathbb{R}\).

\sphinxlineitem{param gamma}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, float, list or numpy.ndarray, optional
GEV shape parameter \(\gamma\in \mathbb{R}\). Default is 0.

\sphinxlineitem{param mu}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, float, list or numpy.ndarray, optional
GEV location parameter \(\mu\in \mathbb{R}\). Default is 0.

\sphinxlineitem{param sigma}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, float, list or numpy.ndarray, optional
GEV scale parameter \(\sigma>0\). Default is 1.

\sphinxlineitem{param theta}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, float, list or numpy.ndarray, optional
Extremal index \(\vartheta\in [0, 1]\). Default is 1.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id15}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{return}
\sphinxAtStartPar
numpy.ndarray or float
The Cumulative Density Function values corresponding to the input \sphinxtitleref{x}.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id16}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{x} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{array}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{GEV\PYGZus{}cdf}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{x}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{gamma}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{mu}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{sigma}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{theta}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.8}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{array([0.54610814, 0.62171922, 0.69703039, 0.77196099, 0.84644106])}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{GEV\_ll() (in module xtremes.miscellaneous)@\spxentry{GEV\_ll()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.miscellaneous}}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.miscellaneous.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{GEV\_ll}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{x}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{gamma}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{mu}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{sigma}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute the log\sphinxhyphen{}likelihood function of the Generalized Extreme Value distribution.


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id17}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes the log\sphinxhyphen{}likelihood function of the Generalized Extreme Value distribution:
\begin{equation*}
\begin{split}l(x) = -\left(1+\gamma \frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}\right)^{-1/\gamma}
       -\frac{\gamma+1}{\gamma}\log\left(1+\gamma \frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}\right)
       -\log\sigma\end{split}
\end{equation*}

\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id18}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param x}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, float, list or numpy.ndarray
GEV argument \(x\in \mathbb{R}\).

\sphinxlineitem{param gamma}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, float, list or numpy.ndarray, optional
GEV shape parameter \(\gamma\in \mathbb{R}\).

\sphinxlineitem{param mu}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, float, list or numpy.ndarray, optional
GEV location parameter \(\mu\in \mathbb{R}\).

\sphinxlineitem{param sigma}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, float, list or numpy.ndarray, optional
GEV scale parameter \(\sigma>0\).

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id19}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{return}
\sphinxAtStartPar
numpy.ndarray or float
The log\sphinxhyphen{}likelihood values corresponding to the input \sphinxtitleref{x}.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id20}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{x} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{array}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{GEV\PYGZus{}ll}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{x}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{gamma}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{mu}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{sigma}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{array([\PYGZhy{}3.11578562, \PYGZhy{}2.23851549, \PYGZhy{}1.85551157, \PYGZhy{}1.57084383, \PYGZhy{}1.33403504])}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\section{Piece Wise Moment Estimation}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:piece-wise-moment-estimation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The following functions are related to Probability Weighted Moment (PWM) estimation:
\index{PWM\_estimation() (in module xtremes.miscellaneous)@\spxentry{PWM\_estimation()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.miscellaneous}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_misc:xtremes.miscellaneous.PWM_estimation}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.miscellaneous.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PWM\_estimation}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{maxima}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
PWM Estimation of GEV parameters.


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id22}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes the first three Probability Weighted Moments \(\beta_0,\beta_1,\beta_2\)
on given block maxima, as introduced in Greenwood et al. (1979).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Let \(M_{(1)} \leq M_{(2)} \leq \cdots \leq M_{(n)}\) be increasingly sorted block maxima.
Then the first three PWMs are defined as:
\begin{equation*}
\begin{split}\beta_0 &:= \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n M_{(i)},\\
\beta_1 &:= \frac{1}{n(n-1)}\sum_{i=1}^n (i-1) M_{(i)},\\
\beta_2 &:= \frac{1}{n(n-1)(n-2)}\sum_{i=1}^n (i-1)(i-2)M_{(i)}.\end{split}
\end{equation*}

\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id23}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param maxima}
\sphinxAtStartPar
list or numpy.array
Sequence of maxima. \sphinxtitleref{len(maxima)} must be greater than or equal to 3.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id24}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{return}
\sphinxAtStartPar
tuple of floats
The first three PWMs: \(\beta_0\), \(\beta_1\), and \(\beta_2\).

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{References}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:references}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Greenwood, J. A., Landwehr, J. M., Matalas, N. C., \& Wallis, J. R. (1979). Probability weighted moments: 
Definition and relation to parameters of several distributions expressible in inverse form. 
Water Resources Research, 15(5), 1049–1054. \sphinxurl{https://doi.org/10.1029/wr015i005p01049}


\subsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id25}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{maxima} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{array}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{8}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{12}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{15}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{18}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{maxima}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{(11.6, 11.2, 39.2)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PWM2GEV() (in module xtremes.miscellaneous)@\spxentry{PWM2GEV()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.miscellaneous}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_misc:xtremes.miscellaneous.PWM2GEV}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.miscellaneous.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PWM2GEV}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{b\_0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{b\_1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{b\_2}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute estimators for the parameters of the GEV distribution from given PWM estimators.


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id26}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes estimators for the parameters of the Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) distribution from given Probability 
Weighted Moment (PWM) estimators. As shown in Hosking et al. (1985), they follow the relationship:
\begin{equation*}
\begin{split}\gamma &= g_1^{-1}\left(\frac{3\beta_2-\beta_0}{2\beta_1-\beta_0}\right), \\
\sigma &= g_2(\gamma)\cdot (2\beta_1-\beta_0), \\
\mu &= \beta_0+\sigma\cdot g_3(\gamma),\end{split}
\end{equation*}
\sphinxAtStartPar
where:
\begin{equation*}
\begin{split}g_1(\gamma) &:= \frac{3^\gamma-1}{2^\gamma-1}, \\
g_2(\gamma) &:= \frac{\gamma}{\Gamma(1-\gamma)(2^\gamma-1)}, \\
g_3(\gamma) &:= \frac{1-\Gamma(1-\gamma)}{\gamma}.\end{split}
\end{equation*}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Note that \(\Gamma\) denotes the gamma function. The values for \(g_\bullet(0)\) 
are defined by continuity to result in:
\begin{equation*}
\begin{split}g_1(0)= \frac{\log 3}{\log 2}, \quad g_2(0) = \frac{1}{\log 2}, \quad g_3(0) = -\gamma_\mathrm{EM},\end{split}
\end{equation*}
\sphinxAtStartPar
with \(\gamma_\mathrm{EM}\) being the Euler\sphinxhyphen{}Mascheroni constant.


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id27}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param b\_0}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int or float
PWM estimator for the first moment.

\sphinxlineitem{param b\_1}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int or float
PWM estimator for the second moment.

\sphinxlineitem{param b\_2}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int or float
PWM estimator for the third moment.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id28}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{return}
\sphinxAtStartPar
tuple of floats
Estimators for the GEV parameters: shape parameter (\(\gamma\)), location parameter (\(\mu\)), 
and scale parameter (\(\sigma\)).

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{References}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id29}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Hosking, J. R. M., Wallis, J. R., \& Wood, E. F. (1985). Estimation of the Generalized Extreme\sphinxhyphen{}Value Distribution 
by the Method of Probability\sphinxhyphen{}Weighted Moments. Technometrics, 27(3), 251–261. 
\sphinxurl{https://doi.org/10.1080/00401706.1985.10488049}


\subsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id30}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{b\PYGZus{}0} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+m+mi}{10}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{b\PYGZus{}1} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+m+mi}{20}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{b\PYGZus{}2} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+m+mi}{30}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{PWM2GEV}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{b\PYGZus{}0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{b\PYGZus{}1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{b\PYGZus{}2}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{(0.289510206281886, 10.446586187753782, 15.207496500178042)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}



\section{Examples}

\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PWM\PYGZus{}estimation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{maxima}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{PWM Estimation Result:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{result}\PYG{p}{)}

\section{Simulating Time Series}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:simulating-time-series}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The following functions are related to simulating time series data:
\index{simulate\_timeseries() (in module xtremes.miscellaneous)@\spxentry{simulate\_timeseries()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.miscellaneous}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_misc:xtremes.miscellaneous.simulate_timeseries}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.miscellaneous.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{simulate\_timeseries}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{n}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{distr}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'GEV'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{correlation}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'IID'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{modelparams}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{{[}0{]}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{ts}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{seed}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Simulate a Time Series for GEV.


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id32}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function allows simulating three different kinds of time series.
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The most basic time series is the IID (independent and identically distributed) case, 
where there is no temporal dependence. The distribution from which the random variables 
are drawn can be chosen via \sphinxtitleref{distr}, and respective model parameters are passed via 
\sphinxtitleref{modelparams}.

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{For a stationary time series with temporal dependence, two models are available:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
ARMAX model: The next value is computed as the maximum of two values: 
\(ts * X_{i-1}\) and \((1 - ts) * Z_i\), where \(Z_i\) is drawn from a GPD (Generalized 
Pareto Distribution) specified by \sphinxtitleref{modelparams}. The parameter \sphinxtitleref{ts} controls the 
temporal dependence.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
AR (Autoregressive) model: Similar to ARMAX, but \(Z_i\) is drawn from a Cauchy distribution.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id33}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param n}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int
Length of time series to simulate.

\sphinxlineitem{param distr}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str, optional
Distribution to draw from. Default is ‘GEV’.

\sphinxlineitem{param correlation}
\sphinxAtStartPar
str, optional
Correlation type to specify, choose from {[}‘IID’, ‘ARMAX’, ‘AR’{]}. Default is ‘IID’.

\sphinxlineitem{param modelparams}
\sphinxAtStartPar
list, optional
Parameters belonging to \sphinxtitleref{distr}. Default is {[}0{]}.

\sphinxlineitem{param ts}
\sphinxAtStartPar
float, optional
Time series parameter \(\alpha\in[0,1]\). Default is 0.

\sphinxlineitem{param seed}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, optional
Random seed for reproducibility. Default is None.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Returns}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id34}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{return}
\sphinxAtStartPar
numpy.ndarray{[}float{]}
Simulated time series.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Raises}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id35}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{ValueError}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If an invalid model is specified.

\end{description}


\subsection{See Also}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:see-also}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Tutorial on Time Series Simulation: \sphinxurl{https://xtremes.readthedocs.io/en/latest/topt.html}


\subsection{Example}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id36}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{simulated\PYGZus{}ts} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{simulate\PYGZus{}timeseries}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{n}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{distr}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{GPD}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{correlation}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{ARMAX}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{modelparams}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.5}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ts}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.7}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{seed}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{42}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{simulated\PYGZus{}ts}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{g+go}{array([0.5791584 , 0.71057555, 0.55079821, 0.61636037, 0.63544943,}
\PYG{g+go}{       0.74830653, 0.66283107, 0.69929097, 0.75026675, 0.69214764])}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{stride2int() (in module xtremes.miscellaneous)@\spxentry{stride2int()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.miscellaneous}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_misc:xtremes.miscellaneous.stride2int}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.miscellaneous.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{stride2int}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{stride}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{block\_size}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Integer from Stride.


\subsection{Notes}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id37}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function is a utility when handling Block maxima (disjoint, sliding, striding).
Apart from giving the stride directly, it is handy to have the additional options
‘SBM’ and ‘DBM’ for sliding and disjoint BM, respectively. This function converts exactly this.


\subsection{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id38}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param stride}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int or str
Stride to be converted.

\sphinxlineitem{param block\_size}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int
Block size for conversion, ignored if stride==’SBM’.

\sphinxlineitem{return}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int
The converted stride.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\subsection{Raises}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id39}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{TypeError}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If stride is not an integer or string.

\end{description}


\subsection{Examples}
\label{\detokenize{api_misc:id40}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{stride2int}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{2}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{stride2int}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{SBM}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{1}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{stride2int}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{DBM}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{10}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

For some models, it is possible to extract the true value
of gamma theoretically. Whenever this is possible, the conversion
should be subject to this function.

list
Valid model parameters.

numpy.ndarray{[}float{]}
Gamma\_true, if applicable. Returns None if gamma\_true cannot be extracted.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\chapter{Bias Correction}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:bias-correction}}\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module computes bias corrections for the Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE) of cluster size distributions in stationary time series. So far, it only works for \$t=2\$.


\section{Overview}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:overview}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{biascorrection} module provides tools for estimating probabilities of cluster sizes, calculating related functions like ( Upsilon ) and ( Pi ), and finding roots for bias correction.


\section{Functions}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:functions}}

\section{The \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{I\_sb}} Function}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:the-i-sb-function}}\index{I\_sb() (in module xtremes.biascorrection)@\spxentry{I\_sb()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.biascorrection}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:xtremes.biascorrection.I_sb}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.biascorrection.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{I\_sb}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{j}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{bs}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate a range of integers representing indices for a sliding block method.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This function is often used in time series analysis or statistical modeling 
contexts where data is divided into overlapping or non\sphinxhyphen{}overlapping blocks.


\subsection{Parameters:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:parameters}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{j}{[}int{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The starting index for the block. This typically represents the position 
in the time series or dataset where the block begins.

\sphinxlineitem{bs}{[}int{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The block size, which determines the number of elements in the block 
and the range of indices generated.

\end{description}


\subsection{Returns:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:returns}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{numpy.ndarray}
\sphinxAtStartPar
An array of consecutive integers starting from \sphinxtitleref{j} and ending at \sphinxtitleref{j + bs \sphinxhyphen{} 1}.
This array can be used to reference indices of elements within a specific block.

\end{description}


\subsection{Example:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:example}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}

\section{The \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{D\_n}} Function}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:the-d-n-function}}\index{D\_n() (in module xtremes.biascorrection)@\spxentry{D\_n()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.biascorrection}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:xtremes.biascorrection.D_n}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.biascorrection.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{D\_n}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{n}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{bs}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate all pairs of indices representing disjoint blocks in a time series.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This function identifies pairs of block indices \sphinxtitleref{(i, j)} such that the blocks 
starting at indices \sphinxtitleref{i} and \sphinxtitleref{j}, each of size \sphinxtitleref{bs}, do not overlap. 
It is useful in time series analysis for constructing disjoint block structures, 
particularly in statistical estimators for cluster sizes.


\subsection{Parameters:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:id1}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{n}{[}int{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The length of the time series or dataset. This determines the range of 
valid indices for block positions.

\sphinxlineitem{bs}{[}int{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The block size, which specifies the number of elements in each block.
Blocks are constructed as contiguous subsets of the time series.

\end{description}


\subsection{Returns:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:id2}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{list of tuples}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A list of tuples \sphinxtitleref{(i, j)}, where \sphinxtitleref{i} and \sphinxtitleref{j} are the starting indices 
of two disjoint blocks. The blocks \sphinxtitleref{I\_sb(i, bs)} and \sphinxtitleref{I\_sb(j, bs)} have 
no overlap, i.e., their intersection is empty.

\end{description}


\subsection{Example:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:id3}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k}{def} \PYG{n+nf}{I\PYGZus{}sb}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{j}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{bs}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }    \PYG{k}{return} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{arange}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{j}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{j} \PYG{o}{+} \PYG{n}{bs}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{D\PYGZus{}n}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{[(1, 3), (1, 4), (2, 4), (3, 1), (3, 2), (4, 1), (4, 2)]}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Notes:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:notes}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The function uses the helper function \sphinxtitleref{I\_sb} to define the range of indices 
covered by a block starting at a given index.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
It checks for disjointness using \sphinxtitleref{np.intersect1d}, which computes the 
intersection of two arrays.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
This function is critical in methods involving disjoint blocks, such as 
certain statistical estimators that assume independence between blocks.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}



\section{The \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{exceedances}} Function}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:the-exceedances-function}}\index{exceedances() (in module xtremes.biascorrection)@\spxentry{exceedances()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.biascorrection}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:xtremes.biascorrection.exceedances}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.biascorrection.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{exceedances}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{data}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{maxima}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{bs}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{i}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{j}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{stride}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'DBM'}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculate the number of exceedances of a given maximum within a specified block.
This function computes the number of values in the \sphinxtitleref{j}\sphinxhyphen{}th block of the data that 
exceed the \sphinxtitleref{i}\sphinxhyphen{}th maximum. The block can be defined using either a disjoint block 
method (DBM) or a sliding block method (SBM).


\subsection{Parameters:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:id4}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{data}{[}array\sphinxhyphen{}like{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The time series or dataset from which exceedances are calculated.

\sphinxlineitem{maxima}{[}array\sphinxhyphen{}like{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
An array of maxima values, where \sphinxtitleref{maxima{[}i\sphinxhyphen{}1{]}} is the reference maximum 
for which exceedances are counted.

\sphinxlineitem{bs}{[}int{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The block size, specifying the number of consecutive elements in each block.

\sphinxlineitem{i}{[}int{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The index (1\sphinxhyphen{}based) of the maximum in \sphinxtitleref{maxima} used as the reference for exceedances.

\sphinxlineitem{j}{[}int{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The index (1\sphinxhyphen{}based) of the block in the dataset to examine for exceedances.

\sphinxlineitem{stride}{[}\{‘DBM’, ‘SBM’\}, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Specifies the block method used:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘DBM’ (Disjoint Block Method): The blocks are non\sphinxhyphen{}overlapping and start at \sphinxtitleref{(j\sphinxhyphen{}1)*bs} and end at \sphinxtitleref{j*bs}.
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘SBM’ (Sliding Block Method): The blocks can overlap and are defined to start at \sphinxtitleref{j} and end at \sphinxtitleref{j+bs}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Default is ‘DBM’.

\end{description}


\subsection{Returns:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:id5}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{int}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The number of elements in the specified block that exceed the given maximum.

\end{description}


\subsection{Example:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:id6}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{data} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{array}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{6}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{7}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{9}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{maxima} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{array}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{7}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{exceedances}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{data}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{maxima}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{bs}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{i}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{j}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{stride}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{DBM}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{1  \PYGZsh{} One value in the 3rd disjoint block exceeds maxima[0] = 5}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{exceedances}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{data}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{maxima}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{bs}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{i}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{j}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{stride}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{SBM}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{2  \PYGZsh{} Two values in the sliding block exceed maxima[1] = 7}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Notes:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:id7}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The stride parameter allows flexibility in block definition:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘DBM’ is suited for non\sphinxhyphen{}overlapping blocks, often used in classical block maxima methods.
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘SBM’ is suited for sliding windows, offering finer granularity for overlap\sphinxhyphen{}based analysis.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Indexing of blocks and maxima is 1\sphinxhyphen{}based for user\sphinxhyphen{}friendliness but is internally converted 
to Python’s 0\sphinxhyphen{}based indexing.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}



\section{The \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{hat\_pi0}} Function}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:the-hat-pi0-function}}\index{hat\_pi0() (in module xtremes.biascorrection)@\spxentry{hat\_pi0()}\spxextra{in module xtremes.biascorrection}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:xtremes.biascorrection.hat_pi0}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xtremes.biascorrection.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{hat\_pi0}}}{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{data}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{maxima}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{bs}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n,n}{stride}\DUrole{o,o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'DBM'}}}{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute estimators ( hat\{pi\}(1) ) for the limiting cluster size probability ( pi(1) ).

\sphinxAtStartPar
This function estimates the first value of ( pi(m) ), which represent probabilities 
associated with cluster sizes in stationary time series.


\subsection{Parameters:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:id8}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{data}{[}array\sphinxhyphen{}like{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The dataset or time series used to compute probabilities and estimators.

\sphinxlineitem{maxima}{[}array\sphinxhyphen{}like, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A pre\sphinxhyphen{}computed array of maxima (e.g., block maxima). If not provided, block maxima 
are computed internally using the specified \sphinxtitleref{bs}.

\sphinxlineitem{bs}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The block size, specifying the number of elements in each block. If not provided, it is 
inferred from the length of \sphinxtitleref{data} divided by the number of maxima, if maxima are provided.

\sphinxlineitem{stride}{[}\{‘DBM’, ‘SBM’\}, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Defines the block method:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘DBM’ (Disjoint Block Method): Non\sphinxhyphen{}overlapping blocks are used.
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘SBM’ (Sliding Block Method): Overlapping sliding blocks are used.
Default is ‘DBM’.

\end{description}


\subsection{Returns:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:id9}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{float}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Estimated ( hat\{pi\}(1) ) value.

\end{description}


\subsection{Raises:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:raises}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{ValueError}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If neither \sphinxtitleref{maxima} nor \sphinxtitleref{bs} is provided.

\end{description}


\subsection{Example:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:id10}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{data} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{array}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{6}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{7}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{9}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{8}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{maxima} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{array}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{7}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{hat\PYGZus{}pis}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{data}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{bs}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{stride}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{SBM}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{[1.5, 1.0]  \PYGZsh{} Using SBM and calculated block maxima}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Notes:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:id11}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The recursive formula for ( hat\{pi\}(m) ) is given by:

\end{itemize}
\begin{equation*}
\begin{split}\hat{\pi}(1) = 4 \bar{p}(1)
\hat{\pi}(m) = 4 \bar{p}(m) - 2 \sum_{k=1}^{m-1} \hat{\pi}(m-k) \bar{p}(k)\end{split}
\end{equation*}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
This ensures that each ( hat\{pi\}(m) ) is built upon the values of smaller ( m ).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
If \sphinxtitleref{pbars} is not provided, the function computes ( bar\{p\}(m) ) using \sphinxtitleref{pbar\_dbm\_fast} 
for the DBM stride. Future extensions can integrate SBM support dynamically.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The function is optimized for the DBM stride but can handle SBM if provided with \sphinxtitleref{pbars}.

\end{itemize}


\subsection{References:}
\label{\detokenize{api_biascorrection:id12}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bücher, A., \& Jennessen, T. (2022). Statistical analysis for stationary time series 
at extreme levels: New estimators for the limiting cluster size distribution. 
Stochastic Processes and their Applications, 149, 75\sphinxhyphen{}106.
